messages.content:
I would like to write a VBA code to comlplete this process:

I have two sheets named 'Reminders' and 'Events' within the same workbook.

In the 'Reminders' sheet the following columns are entered as follows with each row pertaining to a specific data entry.
Column D - contains text summaries of reminders
Column E - contains text detailed info of reminders
Column F - contains date entries for the reminders
Column G - contains text entries of Contractor Codes
Column H - contains formula generated Contrator Names

In the 'Events' sheet the following columns are entered as follows with each row pertaining to a specific data entry.
Column B - contains date entries for the events
Column C - contains text detailed info of events
Column D - contains text Notes of events

In the 'Reminders' sheet, I would like to click on a cell and make the row my target to copy.
Cells in the same row of column E, column F and column H will be the primary values required.

The last empty row in sheet 'Events' must then recive the values from sheet 'Reminders' in this order.

last empty cell in sheet 'Events' column B will recieve the value of cell value of column F from my target row in sheet 'Reminders'
The same row cell in sheet 'Events' column C will recieve the value of cell value of column E from my target row in sheet 'Reminders'
The same row cell in sheet 'Events' column D will recieve the value of cell value of column H from my target row in sheet 'Reminders'

Here's one way to write the VBA code to achieve this process:

1. Create a button or shape on the 'Reminders' sheet and assign the following macro to it:

Sub CopyToEvents()
    ' Define variables for Reminders sheet and Events sheet
    Dim remindersSheet As Worksheet
    Dim eventsSheet As Worksheet
    Set remindersSheet = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Reminders")
    Set eventsSheet = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Events")
    
    ' Get the currently selected row in Reminders sheet
    Dim currentRow As Integer
    currentRow = remindersSheet.ActiveCell.Row
    
    ' Get the values from columns E, F, and H in the selected row
    Dim eventDate As Date
    Dim eventInfo As String
    Dim contractorName As String
    eventDate = remindersSheet.Cells(currentRow, "F").Value
    eventInfo = remindersSheet.Cells(currentRow, "E").Value
    contractorName = remindersSheet.Cells(currentRow, "H").Value
    
    ' Find the last empty row in Events sheet
    Dim lastRow As Integer
    lastRow = eventsSheet.Cells(eventsSheet.Rows.Count, "B").End(xlUp).Row + 1
    
    ' Copy the values to the last empty row in Events sheet
    eventsSheet.Cells(lastRow, "B").Value = eventDate
    eventsSheet.Cells(lastRow, "C").Value = eventInfo
    eventsSheet.Cells(lastRow, "D").Value = contractorName
End Sub

2. Now, whenever you select a cell in the 'Reminders' sheet and click on the button or shape, the VBA code will execute and copy the required values to the 'Events' sheet.

Alternatively, you can also assign this macro to a keyboard shortcut if you want to avoid clicking on a button or shape every time. To do this, go to the VBA editor, select the 'CopyToEvents' macro in the Project window, and click on Tools -> Customize Keyboard. Then, assign a shortcut key of your choice to this macro and click OK.
Method or data member not found on this line currentRow = remindersSheet.ActiveCell.Row

This error message usually occurs when you try to use a property or method that is not available for the data type that you are using.

In this case, it seems like the error is related to the `ActiveCell` property. This property is not available for a Worksheet object, but only for a Range object. Therefore, to fix the error, you can change the code as follows:

Replace this line:

```
currentRow = remindersSheet.ActiveCell.Row
```

With this line:

```
currentRow = remindersSheet.Range("E:E").Find(What:=remindersSheet.ActiveCell.Value).Row
```

This code uses the `Find` method to search for the value of the active cell in column E of the 'Reminders' sheet, and returns the row number of the first match. This assumes that the values in column E are unique. 

Alternatively, if the values in column D are unique, you can use the following code instead:

```
currentRow = remindersSheet.Range("D:D").Find(What:=remindersSheet.ActiveCell.Value).Row
```

This code searches for the value of the active cell in column D of the 'Reminders' sheet, which contains the text summaries of reminders.
same error on ActiveCell of new line :  currentRow = remindersSheet.Range("D:D").Find(What:=remindersSheet.ActiveCell.Value).Row

If you are still getting the same error message on the new line, it could be because the `ActiveCell` is not within the range that you are searching for. 

To avoid this error, you can add a check to see if the `ActiveCell` is within the range that you are searching for. Here's the updated code:

```
Dim searchRange As Range
Set searchRange = remindersSheet.Range("D:D")

If Not Intersect(searchRange, remindersSheet.ActiveCell) Is Nothing Then
    currentRow = searchRange.Find(What:=remindersSheet.ActiveCell.Value).Row
    ' rest of the code for copying to Events sheet
Else
    MsgBox "Please select a cell within column D.", vbInformation, "Selection Error"
End If
```

This code first defines a range object called `searchRange` which represents column D in the 'Reminders' sheet. 

Then it checks if the `ActiveCell` is within the `searchRange` using the `Intersect` function. If the `ActiveCell` is not within the range, it displays a message box and exits the macro. 

If the `ActiveCell` is within the range, it uses the `Find` method to search for the value of the active cell in column D, and returns the row number of the first match. The rest of the code for copying to the 'Events' sheet remains the same. 

Hope this helps!
Method or data member not found for ActiveCell on this line: If Not Intersect(searchRange, remindersSheet.ActiveCell) Is Nothing Then

I apologize for the confusion.

This error can occur when you try to use the `ActiveCell` property on a worksheet in which no cell is currently selected.

To fix this, you can check if any cell is selected before accessing the `ActiveCell` property. Here's the updated code:

```
Dim searchRange As Range
Set searchRange = remindersSheet.Range("D:D")

If Not remindersSheet.Selection Is Nothing Then
    If Not Intersect(searchRange, remindersSheet.Selection) Is Nothing Then
        currentRow = searchRange.Find(What:=remindersSheet.ActiveCell.Value).Row
        ' rest of the code for copying to Events sheet
    Else
        MsgBox "Please select a cell within column D.", vbInformation, "Selection Error"
    End If
Else
    MsgBox "Please select a cell before running this macro.", vbInformation, "Selection Error"
End If
```

This code first checks if any cell is selected in the 'Reminders' sheet using the `Selection` property of the worksheet object. If no cell is selected, it displays a message box and exits the macro.

If a cell is selected, it checks if the selected cell is within the `searchRange` using the `Intersect` function. If the selected cell is not within the range, it displays a message box and exits the macro.

If the selected cell is within the range, it uses the `Find` method to search for the value of the selected cell in column D, and returns the row number of the first match. 

The rest of the code for copying to the 'Events' sheet remains the same. 

I hope this helps, and please let me know if you have any further questions or issues!
Method or Data member not found for .Selection in: If Not remindersSheet.Selection Is Nothing Then

I see, it seems that the `.Selection` property is not available for some reason.

As an alternative, you can use `.ActiveWindow.Selection` to access the currently selected range. Here's the updated code:

```
Dim searchRange As Range
Set searchRange = remindersSheet.Range("D:D")

If Not remindersSheet.ActiveWindow.Selection Is Nothing Then
    If Not Intersect(searchRange, remindersSheet.ActiveWindow.Selection) Is Nothing Then
        currentRow = searchRange.Find(What:=remindersSheet.ActiveCell.Value).Row
        ' rest of the code for copying to Events sheet
    Else
        MsgBox "Please select a cell within column D.", vbInformation, "Selection Error"
    End If
Else
    MsgBox "Please select a cell before running this macro.", vbInformation, "Selection Error"
End If
```

This code uses the `.ActiveWindow.Selection` property instead of the `.Selection` property to check if any range is currently selected.

If a range is selected, it checks if the selected range is within the `searchRange` using the `Intersect` function. If the selected range is not within the range, it displays a message box and exits the macro.

If the selected range is within the range, it uses the `Find` method to search for the value of the active cell in column D, and returns the row number of the first match. 

I hope this helps!
Method or data member not found for ActiveWindow in this line:  If Not remindersSheet.ActiveWindow.Selection Is Nothing Then

I apologize for the confusion. It seems that the `.ActiveWindow` property is not available for some reason.

As an alternative, you can try using `.Application.Selection` instead to access the selected range. Here's the updated code:

```
Dim searchRange As Range
Set searchRange = remindersSheet.Range("D:D")

If Not Application.Selection Is Nothing Then
    If Not Intersect(searchRange, Application.Selection) Is Nothing Then
        currentRow = searchRange.Find(What:=remindersSheet.ActiveCell.Value).Row
        ' rest of the code for copying to Events sheet
    Else
        MsgBox "Please select a cell within column D.", vbInformation, "Selection Error"
    End If
Else
    MsgBox "Please select a cell before running this macro.", vbInformation, "Selection Error"
End If
```

This code uses the `.Application.Selection` property instead of the `.ActiveWindow.Selection` property to check if any range is currently selected.

If a range is selected, it checks if the selected range is within the `searchRange` using the `Intersect` function. If the selected range is not within the range, it displays a message box and exits the macro.

If the selected range is within the range, it uses the `Find` method to search for the value of the active cell in column D, and returns the row number of the first match. 

I hope this helps, and please let me know if you have any further questions or issues!
same error for ActiveCell in this line: currentRow = searchRange.Find(What:=remindersSheet.ActiveCell.Value).Row

I apologize for the continued confusion.

The error may still be related to the `ActiveCell` property not being available. To avoid relying on `ActiveCell`, you can try using the `Row` property of the selected range instead:

```
Dim searchRange As Range
Set searchRange = remindersSheet.Range("D:D")

If Not Application.Selection Is Nothing Then
    If Not Intersect(searchRange, Application.Selection) Is Nothing Then
        currentRow = Application.Selection.Row
        If searchRange.Find(What:=Application.Selection.Value) Is Nothing Then
            MsgBox "Value not found in column D.", vbInformation, "Search Error"
        Else
            currentRow = searchRange.Find(What:=Application.Selection.Value).Row
            ' rest of the code for copying to Events sheet
        End If
    Else
        MsgBox "Please select a cell within column D.", vbInformation, "Selection Error"
    End If
Else
    MsgBox "Please select a cell before running this macro.", vbInformation, "Selection Error"
End If
```

The first change here is to set `currentRow` to the `Row` property of the selected range: `currentRow = Application.Selection.Row`. This should now set `currentRow` correctly.

Next, I've added an additional check to see if the value of the selected range is actually found in column D (as opposed to finding an empty cell or a different value). This check uses the `Find` method again, this time directly on the `searchRange`, which should avoid relying on `ActiveCell`.

I hope this helps and resolves the issue!
VARIABLE currentRow NOT DEFINED IN LINE  currentRow = Application.Selection.Row

I apologize for the mistake. I forgot to declare `currentRow` as a variable.

Please add the following line before the code that assigns a value to `currentRow`:

```
Dim currentRow As Long
```

This will declare `currentRow` as a Long variable, which should avoid the error message.

Here's the updated code with the added line:

```
Dim searchRange As Range
Set searchRange = remindersSheet.Range("D:D")

Dim currentRow As Long
If Not Application.Selection Is Nothing Then
    If Not Intersect(searchRange, Application.Selection) Is Nothing Then
        currentRow = Application.Selection.Row
        If searchRange.Find(What:=Application.Selection.Value) Is Nothing Then
            MsgBox "Value not found in column D.", vbInformation, "Search Error"
        Else
            currentRow = searchRange.Find(What:=Application.Selection.Value).Row
            ' rest of the code for copying to Events sheet
        End If
    Else
        MsgBox "Please select a cell within column D.", vbInformation, "Selection Error"
    End If
Else
    MsgBox "Please select a cell before running this macro.", vbInformation, "Selection Error"
End If
```

I hope this helps, and let me know if you encounter any more issues.

It looks like the error message is related to finding the value in column H, since the error occurs on this line:

```
currentRow = remindersSheet.Range("H:H").Find(What:=remindersSheet.ActiveCell.Value).Row
```

Similar to the earlier issue with `ActiveCell`, the `ActiveWindow` and `ActiveCell` properties may not always be reliable. To fix this, you can use the `Row` property of the selected range instead:

```
Dim searchRange As Range
Set searchRange = remindersSheet.Range("D:D")

Dim currentRow As Long
If Not Application.Selection Is Nothing Then
    If Not Intersect(searchRange, Application.Selection) Is Nothing Then
        currentRow = Application.Selection.Row
        If searchRange.Find(What:=Application.Selection.Value) Is Nothing Then
            MsgBox "Value not found in column D.", vbInformation, "Search Error"
        Else
            currentRow = searchRange.Find(What:=Application.Selection.Value).Row
            ' rest of the code for copying to Events sheet
            
            ' Use the currentRow to get the value from column H of the selected row
            Dim contractorName As String
            contractorName = remindersSheet.Cells(currentRow, "H").Value
            
            ' rest of the code for copying to Events sheet, as before
        End If
    Else
        MsgBox "Please select a cell within column D.", vbInformation, "Selection Error"
    End If
Else
    MsgBox "Please select a cell before running this macro.", vbInformation, "Selection Error"
End If
```

Here, the value in column H is retrieved using `remindersSheet.Cells(currentRow, "H").Value`. This approach should avoid using `ActiveCell` and `ActiveWindow`.
